title: Cost-aware no-trade rebalancing band for the survival book (~0.05 L1)
source: CEO 2-step plan 2026-05-31, step (2). Motivated by B0117: assets are mildly MEAN-REVERTING (VR<1), not trending, so the mild reversion is best harvested PASSIVELY via lazy rebalancing rather than as an active signal. Theory: with proportional costs the optimal rebalancing policy is an INACTION REGION (Davis & Norman 1990; Shreve & Soner 1994), half-width ~ cost^(1/3).
why: The book rebalances on a WEEKLY CALENDAR and pays turnover on every small drift. A no-trade band skips the rebalance when L1 weight drift < threshold, cutting cost drag with NO directional bet — on a low-vol preservation book (Sharpe ~0.5, vol ~2.5%) halving the cost drag is a real, permanent net-Sharpe improvement.
value_rationale: M: a free (edge-independent) net-Sharpe improvement and the ONLY positive result of the 2026-05-31 alpha-frontier sweep; bounded, parity-safe, deployable on the competition venue. Not H only because the absolute cost drag is already small (~0.10%/yr), so the lift is real but modest.
scope: DONE (measurement): parity-safe no_trade_band param in scripts/backtest_survival_book run_backtest (0.0 == byte-identical, verified vs backtest.json + unit test) + a pre-registered band sweep (scripts/sweep_no_trade_band.py).
TODO (deploy, risk-officer-gated): wire the equivalent band into the LIVE loop (execution/live_loop.py reconcile step) so live==backtest; arm band~0.05.
recommendation: PROMOTE band~0.05 to a spec + risk-officer review, then wire into the live loop. Evidence (scripts/sweep_no_trade_band.py, crypto-era + full history, robust PLATEAU not peak): band=0.05 cuts total cost drag -47% (crypto: 0.100%->0.053%) / -58% (full: 0.120%->0.050%) with dSharpe -0.005 / +0.000 and maxDD NON-worse (slightly better) in both regimes; rebalances 261->32 / 530->36. band=0.10 cuts more cost but dSharpe starts to bite (-0.053 crypto) -> 0.05 is the plateau. Deploy step must add the band to LiveLoop.run_cycle's reconcile and keep the live==backtest parity test green.
out_of_scope: Active mean-reversion trading (B0117, discarded); to-the-band-edge partial rebalancing (this is skip-or-full); per-asset (vs L1-portfolio) bands.

no-trade band design (risk-officer must-fixes folded in)

history.reason: Spec written + risk-officer design review (PROCEED_WITH_CAVEAT) + MECHANISM BUILT & TESTED (default OFF). Risk-officer caught a high-severity design gap: the cap-bypass must check the HELD book, not the always-compliant target. FIXED: new pure fn pipeline.survival_book.held_book_cap_breach (per-asset/gross/stressed-crypto-var on an independent conservative cov) gates the band; wired into LiveLoop.run_cycle (execution/live_loop.py) as an execution-layer skip downstream of the SSOT target; on a HOLD, positions persist from BROKER TRUTH (must-fix). SurvivalBookConfig.no_trade_band default 0.0 (parity-safe, NOT armed). Tests: tests/test_no_trade_band.py (7) + tests/test_survival_book.py::test_no_trade_band_parity_and_cost_reduction; full suite 1178 passing incl. the live==backtest parity test. REMAINING (CEO deploy decision, not engineering): arm no_trade_band=0.02 in the live run config, step to 0.05 only after a drift-aware sweep. Mechanism done; arming is the load-bearing CEO checkpoint.
feat(survival): B0118 live no-trade band â€” mechanism built (default OFF), risk-reviewed

history.reason: DRIFT-AWARE pre-arm validation (scripts/sweep_no_trade_band_driftaware.py) — closes the risk-officer's caveat that the constant-weight sweep didn't reflect the live (price-drifted) held book. Held weights drift buy-and-hold between rebalances; runs the EXACT deployed logic (band + held_book_cap_breach bypass + DD latch); tracks the worst HELD-book risk. CRYPTO-ERA (deployed regime, n_reb=261 EXACTLY matches run_backtest): worst held crypto var share = 0.473 IDENTICAL at band=0.00/0.02/0.05 -> the band does NOT worsen the held risk profile (it is a pre-existing weekly-rebalance drift property, present at band=0); worst held gross 0.504 (all); maxDD -5.02%/-5.01% vs baseline -5.17% (NON-worse); dSharpe -0.005/-0.009 (negligible); held-cap bypass FIRED 33-38x (working as designed). Cost cut is REAL but MORE MODEST than the constant-weight sweep: -17.5% (0.02) / -37.0% (0.05), not -23%/-47% (risk-officer was right that constant-weight overstated). VERDICT: arming is SAFE in the deployed regime at both 0.02 and 0.05 (identical held risk, non-worse maxDD, ~0 dSharpe). CAVEAT: full-history drift-aware is anomalous (pre-crypto sparse-data era churns flat<->invested) and is NOT the arming basis — crypto-era is. Spawned B0119 (pre-existing intra-week crypto var drift to ~47%). Arming value remains a CEO decision.
research(survival): B0118 drift-aware band validation â€” arm-safe; spawns B0119

ARMED at 0.05 in the deployed runner (CEO decision 2026-05-31, after the drift-aware validation cleared the risk-officer's pre-arm caveat). scripts/run_survival_live.py: --no-trade-band default 0.05 -> SurvivalBookConfig.no_trade_band; dry-run cycle confirms the band is live (banner shows 'no-trade band: 0.050'); 0.0 disables, 0.02 conservative. Tested: test_run_survival_live_args (armed-at-0.05 + override) + tests/test_no_trade_band.py (held-cap guard, parity, hold, latch-bypass, over-cap bypass) + the parity unit test; full suite green. Net effect in the deployed crypto regime (drift-aware): cost drag -37%, dSharpe ~0, maxDD non-worse, held risk profile IDENTICAL to baseline (held-cap bypass enforces it). The mechanism default stays 0.0 (parity-safe) at the SurvivalBookConfig level; only the live RUNNER arms 0.05. Follow-up B0119 (intra-week crypto var drift) tracked separately.
feat(survival): B0118 ARM no-trade band at 0.05 in the live runner (CEO 2026-05-31)
docs(survival): document the no-trade band (B0118) in the live-strategy explainer

RE-ARMED 0.05 -> 0.02 (CEO 2026-05-31). 0.02 is the conservative value for the hackathon window (15-27 jun 2026): the band's cost benefit accrues over months not a ~2-week contest, while a tighter band keeps the book closest to its audited min-vol weights for a short variance-contest. Drift-aware shows 0.02 and 0.05 have ~identical risk (dSharpe ~0, maxDD non-worse, held profile identical); 0.02 cost cut -17.5%. run_survival_live --no-trade-band default now 0.02; revert to 0.05 (product-optimal) post-contest via the flag. No code change beyond the default + test + doc.
research(survival): B0119 quantify intra-week crypto var drift â€” centered at the cap
